compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
On every autodeploy restart the review-bot silently abandons acknowledged-but-unreviewed
webhooks (the in-memory review queue is dropped), so changes stall until the slow backfill
reconciler catches up — and the reconciler itself can be frozen indefinitely by one hung
review. Drain the queue on graceful shutdown and give backfill reviews the same per-review
timeout the live worker has.

## Why
The review queue is an in-memory `asyncio.Queue` (`app.py:107`). `POST /webhook` enqueues and
returns `202 {"queued": true}` — a promise to process the event. But the lifespan shutdown
(`app.py:127-134`) **cancels the worker without draining the queue**, so on the hourly
autodeploy restart (ADR-0026) any enqueued-but-unreviewed event is lost, and any in-flight
review is cancelled. Recovery falls entirely to the reconcile backfill, which is slow (300s
interval; sequential full-LLM-review per change; cursor advanced only at end-of-pass,
`reconcile.py:221`). This was observed live: an autodeploy at ~22:53Z dropped a batch of
in-flight webhooks (changes 716-723) that then took ~25 min to drain. Separately, the worker
wraps each review in `asyncio.wait_for(..., timeout)` (`app.py:153`) but `reconcile_once` calls
`review_and_vote` with NO timeout (`reconcile.py:215`) — so a single hung backfill review would
freeze ALL backfill indefinitely (the safety-net has no safety-net).

## What
1. **Graceful drain (app.py lifespan):** in the shutdown `finally`, BEFORE cancelling the
   worker, `await asyncio.wait_for(queue.join(), timeout=SHUTDOWN_DRAIN_SECONDS)` (suppressing
   TimeoutError) so queued events are processed by the still-running worker before exit;
   anything not drained in the window still falls to the reconciler (fail-safe, not fail-lose).
   `SHUTDOWN_DRAIN_SECONDS` from env (default 45).
2. **stop_grace_period (docker-compose.yml):** set the review-bot service `stop_grace_period`
   to 60s so Docker's SIGTERM→SIGKILL window is long enough for the drain to run (default 10s
   would SIGKILL mid-drain).
3. **Reconciler timeout parity (reconcile.py):** wrap the `review_and_vote` call in
   `reconcile_once` in `asyncio.wait_for(..., timeout=<review_timeout>)`; on TimeoutError emit a
   greppable marker and CONTINUE to the next candidate (never freeze the loop). Share the
   timeout value with the worker (factor `review_timeout_seconds()` so both read the same
   `REVIEW_TIMEOUT_SECONDS`).

## Scope
- `src/rebar/review_bot/app.py`: drain in lifespan `finally`; `SHUTDOWN_DRAIN_SECONDS` helper.
- `src/rebar/review_bot/reconcile.py`: per-review `wait_for` + timeout marker + continue.
- `src/rebar/review_bot/config.py` (or a small shared helper): `review_timeout_seconds()` used
  by both app and reconcile (DRY; avoids an app<->reconcile import cycle).
- `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml`: `stop_grace_period: 60s` on `review-bot`.
- `tests/unit/test_review_bot.py`: RED tests for the drain and the reconciler timeout.
- Non-goal: replacing the in-memory queue with a persistent/disk-backed queue (heavier; the
  reconciler remains the ultimate recovery path — chosen approach per operator direction).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] On lifespan shutdown with items still queued, the items are drained (processed by the
      worker) rather than dropped; before the fix they are dropped.
      Proving command: `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_review_bot.py -k shutdown_drain -q`
      passes and is confirmed RED on the pre-fix tree.
- [ ] The drain is bounded: if draining exceeds `SHUTDOWN_DRAIN_SECONDS`, shutdown still
      completes (does not hang) and remaining items are left for the reconciler.
      Proving command: a test with a slow/blocking worker asserts shutdown returns within the
      bound.
- [ ] `reconcile_once` does not hang on a review that never returns: a review exceeding the
      timeout is abandoned, a marker is emitted, and the loop proceeds to the next candidate.
      Proving command: `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_review_bot.py -k reconcile_timeout -q`
      passes and is confirmed RED on the pre-fix tree.
- [ ] `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` sets `stop_grace_period` on the review-bot service.
      Proving command: `python -c "import yaml; d=yaml.safe_load(open('infra/compose/docker-compose.yml')); assert d['services']['review-bot'].get('stop_grace_period')"`
- [ ] Existing gates stay green.
      Proving command: `make lint && make typecheck && python -m pytest tests/unit/test_review_bot.py -q` all exit 0.

file_impact.reason: RED tests for drain + reconcile timeout

compiled_state.title: review-bot: drain review queue on shutdown + reconciler per-review timeout (deploy resilience)
